camera: Straight top-down (bird's-eye) view, ship's nose pointing up toward the top of the frame, centered and filling most of the frame.
style: Detailed 16-bit-era pixel art, in the style of classic top-down shoot-'em-up sprites (Ikaruga, Raiden). Crisp hard-edged shapes, no soft gradients or airbrushed shading — use simple banded/dithered shading for depth instead. Limited, deliberate color palette (roughly 8-16 colors per ship). Strong, clean, iconic silhouette that reads instantly even at small size.
palette: Exact colors vary per ship/faction — always specified in the asset description below, not fixed here. Keep each faction's colors saturated and high-contrast against the dark background.
background: Simple, smooth radial gradient from dark navy (#2f3b52) to near-black — no stars, no nebula, no other objects — so the asset reads clearly as a sprite at small sizes.
constraints: Square 1:1 composition. No text, no logos, no watermarks, no UI elements, no drop shadow outside the subject.
